# Qwik

> Qwik is a resumable web framework. It serializes application state into HTML during SSR and resumes on the client without hydration — the app is instantly interactive regardless of size. The developer experience is very similar to React. This site holds documentation, guides, an interactive playground, and more.

## Quick start

Qwik uses JSX and components like React, with two key differences: components are wrapped in `component$()`, and any function passed to the framework that might run later uses a `$` suffix. The `$` marks a lazy-loading boundary — the Rust-based optimizer extracts these into separate chunks so only the code that's actually needed gets downloaded.

```tsx
import { component$, useSignal } from '@qwik.dev/core';

export default component$(() => {
  const count = useSignal(0);
  return <button onClick$={() => count.value++}>{count.value}</button>;
});
```

- [Getting Started](/docs/getting-started/)
- [React Cheat Sheet](/docs/guides/react-cheat-sheet/)
- [Playground](/playground/)

## Core concepts

**Resumability:** Unlike hydration-based frameworks, Qwik serializes all state and event bindings into HTML. The client resumes from that snapshot — component code only loads when triggered by user interaction.

**The `$` sign:** Functions ending with `$` (like `component$`, `useTask$`, `$()`) create QRL boundaries. The optimizer extracts each into its own lazy-loaded chunk. Variables captured inside a `$` closure must be serializable.

**Fine-grained signals:** Reactive state (`useSignal`, `useStore`) updates only the specific DOM nodes that read it — no virtual DOM diffing, no re-rendering entire component trees.

- [Think Qwik](/docs/concepts/think-qwik/)
- [Resumability](/docs/concepts/resumable/)
- [Reactivity](/docs/concepts/reactivity/)
- [The $ sign](/docs/advanced/dollar/)
- [QRL](/docs/advanced/qrl/)

## Components and state (React equivalents)

- `component$(() => JSX)` — define a component (like a React function component)
- `useSignal(initial)` — reactive primitive value (like `useState`)
- `useStore(obj)` — reactive object/array (like `useState` with objects, but deeply reactive)
- `useComputed$(() => value)` — derived value (like `useMemo`)
- `useAsync$(() => promise)` — async derived value, can stream results
- `useTask$(({track}) => {})` — runs on mount and when tracked values change, on both server and client (like `useEffect` but also runs during SSR)
- `useVisibleTask$(() => {})` — runs only in the browser when the component becomes visible (like `useEffect`)
- `useResource$(({track}) => promise)` — resource with loading/error states
- `useContext(ctx)` / `useContextProvider(ctx, value)` — context (like React context)
- `Slot` — projected content (like React `children`)
- `$(() => {})` — create a standalone QRL (lazy-loadable function reference)

- [Components](/docs/core/overview/)
- [State](/docs/core/state/)
- [Tasks & Lifecycle](/docs/core/tasks/)
- [Context](/docs/core/context/)
- [Slots](/docs/core/slots/)

## Events and DOM

Event handlers use the `on` prefix with `$` suffix: `onClick$={() => {}}`. Handlers are lazy-loaded — they only download when the user actually interacts.

- `useOn('event', $(() => {}))` — programmatic event listener on host element
- `useOnDocument('event', $(() => {}))` — listener on `document`
- `useOnWindow('event', $(() => {}))` — listener on `window`
- `useStyles$(css)` / `useStylesScoped$(css)` — inject styles (scoped version uses attribute selectors)
- `useErrorBoundary()` — catch rendering errors

- [Events](/docs/core/events/)
- [Styling](/docs/core/styles/)
- [Rendering](/docs/core/rendering/)

## Router (meta-framework)

Qwik Router (`@qwik.dev/router`) provides file-based routing, server-side data loading, and actions — similar to Next.js or Remix.

- `routeLoader$(() => data)` — load data on the server before rendering (co-located with the route)
- `routeAction$(() => result)` — handle form submissions / mutations on the server
- `server$(() => result)` — RPC-style server function callable from client code
- `Form` component — progressively enhanced form (works without JS)
- `useLocation()` — current URL and route params
- `useNavigate()` — programmatic navigation

- [Routing](/docs/routing/)
- [Route Loaders](/docs/route-loader/)
- [Actions](/docs/action/)
- [server$](/docs/server$/)
- [Project Structure](/docs/project-structure/)
